skill.name: flux-kontext
skill.description: Edit images with Flux 1 Kontext Pro (Black Forest Labs' precise local image-edit model) on RunComfy — bundled with the model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper output than naive prompting against the same model. Documents Flux Kontext's strengths (single-reference precise local edits, strong prompt control, consistent high-fidelity outputs), the schema (single image + prompt), and when to route to Nano Banana Edit / GPT Image 2 edit / Flux 2 Klein instead. Calls `runcomfy run blackforestlabs/flux-1-kontext/pro/edit` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "flux kontext", "flux-kontext", "flux 1 kontext", "kontext", "BFL kontext", or any explicit ask to edit with this model.
skill.summary: Precise local image editing through Black Forest Labs' Flux 1 Kontext Pro on the RunComfy CLI with documented prompting patterns.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: All 53 static analysis findings are false positives. The static analyzer incorrectly flagged Markdown backtick formatting (36 instances) as Ruby shell backtick execution, documentation URLs (9 instances) as malicious network calls, placeholder text like '<absolute/path>' (2 instances) as path traversal, documentation about token storage location (2 instances) as hidden file access, and plain text table content (3 instances) as weak cryptographic algorithms. The skill is a transparently-documented wrapper for the RunComfy CLI to invoke Flux Kontext Pro image editing. It uses legitimate external commands (runcomfy CLI), documented network endpoints (runcomfy.com), and normal filesystem writes (output directory). The skill includes a detailed security and privacy section documenting token storage, input boundaries, outbound endpoints, and file size limits.

low_findings.title: Markdown code blocks treated as shell execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 36 occurrences of Markdown backtick formatting as Ruby/shell backtick execution. All instances are inside Markdown code blocks (bash), inline code spans, or plain text documentation. No actual shell execution occurs in the skill file itself.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs flagged as network threats
low_findings.description: All 9 hardcoded URL findings point to legitimate RunComfy platform URLs (homepage, model page, documentation). No data exfiltration or malicious network targets are present.

low_findings.title: Placeholder text flagged as path traversal
low_findings.description: The string '<absolute/path>' in bash code examples was flagged as a path traversal sequence. It is a documentation placeholder indicating where the user should specify an output directory.

low_findings.title: Token storage documentation flagged as hidden file access
low_findings.description: The path '~/.config/runcomfy/token.json' appears in the Security and Privacy section documenting how the RunComfy CLI stores API tokens with mode 0600. This is transparent security documentation, not malicious access.

low_findings.title: Plain text flagged as weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: Three instances of plain text table content and YAML frontmatter were incorrectly flagged as weak cryptographic algorithms. None of the flagged locations contain any cryptographic code.

low_findings.title: Routing logic flagged as system reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Line 41 describes routing logic: 'If the user said Flux Kontext / kontext / BFL Kontext explicitly, route here regardless.' This is trigger documentation for the AI assistant, not system reconnaissance.

content.user_title: Edit Images with Flux 1 Kontext Pro on RunComfy
content.value_statement: AI image editing often produces low-fidelity results or requires complex multi-step workflows. This skill provides precise single-reference local image editing through Black Forest Labs' Flux 1 Kontext Pro model with documented prompting patterns for consistent high-quality output.
actual_capabilities: Run precise single-reference local image edits through the Flux 1 Kontext Pro model
Generate reproducible edits with seed parameters for variant comparison
Edit specific objects, text, and composition elements while preserving source identity
Handle aspect ratio selection from supported options for output formatting
Integrate with RunComfy CLI for authenticated model API access
limitations: Accepts only one source image per edit — does not support multi-image or batch editing
Compound prompts with multiple simultaneous changes cause output drift
Cannot generate novel compositions without a source image — use text-to-image models instead
RunComfy CLI and account setup required before first use

use_cases.title: Product Image Polish for E-Commerce
use_cases.description: Edit product photos by replacing text on labels, changing colors, or adding objects while preserving the original product silhouette and lighting.

use_cases.title: Creative Portrait Adjustments
use_cases.description: Add accessories, change expressions, or modify background elements in portrait photos while preserving the person's face, pose, and clothing.

use_cases.title: Brand Asset Text and Color Swaps
use_cases.description: Replace brand text or swap colors on marketing assets with precise preservation-led prompting, keeping the original layout and design intact.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Single-Object Edit
prompt_templates.prompt: Add [object description] to the image. Keep everything else unchanged.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user making a basic edit to one image

prompt_templates.title: Preservation-Led Targeted Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the [key elements] exactly as in the input. Replace only [specific element] from [old value] to [new value]. Same style, position, and size.
prompt_templates.scenario: Editing a specific detail while preserving surrounding context

prompt_templates.title: Compositional Micro-Edit with Seed
prompt_templates.prompt: Keep the [person's identity, pose, clothing] unchanged. Add [new element, color, position]. Use seed [number] for reproducible output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Adding a new object to a scene with repeatable results

prompt_templates.title: Sequential Multi-Pass Edit Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Pass 1: Keep everything unchanged except [first change]. Pass 2: Using the output from pass 1, keep everything unchanged except [second change]. This avoids drift from compound prompts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Making multiple changes to one image by splitting into sequential single-instruction passes

output_examples.input: A product photo of a wine bottle on a wooden table. Request: Replace the brand label text from OLD to NEW while keeping the bottle silhouette, table, and lighting exactly the same.
output_examples.output: The skill produces an edited image where only the label text has changed from OLD to NEW. The bottle shape, table surface, wood grain, and lighting remain identical to the source. The new text matches the original font weight, color contrast, and positioning.

output_examples.input: A portrait photo of a person standing outdoors. Request: Add a dark brown leather shoulder bag hanging on the right shoulder while preserving the person's face, pose, clothing, and background.
output_examples.output: The skill produces an edited portrait where a leather shoulder bag appears on the right shoulder. The person's facial features, expression, clothing details, and background scene are preserved with high fidelity. The bag integrates naturally with the lighting and shadows of the original scene.

best_practices: Lead every prompt with preservation instructions stating what must stay unchanged, then describe the single change you want
Split complex multi-element edits into sequential single-instruction passes to avoid output drift
Use a fixed seed number when you need to compare multiple edit variations on the same source image
anti_patterns: Writing compound prompts that request multiple simultaneous changes — this causes the model to drift from the source image
Using passive voice in edit instructions instead of direct declarative statements like 'Add X' or 'Replace Y with Z'
Attempting to use multiple source images with Flux Kontext when it only supports single-image input

faq.question: What is Flux 1 Kontext Pro?
faq.answer: It is Black Forest Labs' image editing model designed for single-reference precise local edits. It maintains high fidelity to the source image while applying targeted changes based on a text prompt.

faq.question: Do I need a RunComfy account to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need the RunComfy CLI installed and authenticated. Run 'runcomfy login' to start a browser-based device code flow for authentication.

faq.question: Can I edit multiple images at once?
faq.answer: No. Flux 1 Kontext Pro accepts only one source image per edit. For batch edits across 1 to 20 images, use the Nano Banana Edit skill instead.

faq.question: Why do my edits sometimes look wrong or distorted?
faq.answer: Compound prompts with multiple changes often cause output drift. Split your edit into sequential single-instruction passes. Each pass should change only one element at a time.

faq.question: How do I get reproducible results?
faq.answer: Include a fixed seed number in your request. The same seed with the same prompt and source image produces consistent outputs for reliable variant comparison.

faq.question: Can Flux Kontext generate images from scratch?
faq.answer: No. It requires a source image to edit. For generating new images without a source, use the Flux 2 Klein text-to-image model instead.
